2024-09-20.thesis: Microsoft signs 20-yr PPA to restart Three Mile Island nuclear plant
2024-09-20.exit_case: PPA was signed September 20 — the specific named deal-signing catalyst has already occurred and stocks already popped.
2024-09-20.assessment: Microsoft's 20-year PPA with Constellation Energy to restart Three Mile Island was signed and publicly announced on September 20, 2024 — the exact entry date. The Motley Fool confirms CEG, NuScale, Nano Nuclear, and Oklo all popped on the news that day. The specific catalyst as stated — 'Microsoft signs 20-yr PPA' — is a named deal-closing event, and it has now closed. Downstream events such as NRC license reinstatement and the actual restart of TMI Unit 1 (expected ~2028) are real and concrete, but they are NEW catalysts not entered on. The edge of positioning ahead of this specific announcement is gone. The instructions are unambiguous: 'named deal closed' = catalyst_resolved. Exit the position; do not conflate the broader nuclear renaissance theme with the specific catalyst entered on.
2024-09-20.exit_advice: Exit CEG. The stated catalyst is 'Microsoft signs 20-yr PPA' — a named deal closing — and it closed on entry day (Sept 20). The immediate pop is already in. Per the exit rule, a named deal signed/closed resolves the catalyst regardless of downstream events (NRC reinstatement, actual TMI restart) that were not the entered catalyst.

2024-10-18.thesis: Amazon signs named SMR deals with X Energy and Energy Northwest
2024-10-18.exit_case: Amazon's named SMR deals with X Energy and Energy Northwest were already signed and announced ~Oct 16 — a closed, discrete event.
2024-10-18.assessment: The fixed catalyst (Amazon signing named SMR deals with X Energy and Energy Northwest) resolved on or around October 16, 2024 — two days before entry. By entry date the event is already a closed, publicly-announced discrete signing, covered wall-to-wall by IBD ('Amazon Goes Nuclear; Stock of the Day Cameco Explodes'), Benzinga, Motley Fool, Forbes, and Yahoo Finance. The CCJ pop has already occurred. Coverage is mainstream-saturated on day one of this position. No concrete, scheduled follow-on catalyst is identified in the news — there is no named NRC filing date, no construction-start milestone, no additional signed agreements pending; only the ambient AI-energy demand theme persists, which is speculative and generic, not a datable catalyst. Both exit conditions are met simultaneously: (1) the catalyst arc is complete (the deal is signed, the press has processed it), and (2) coverage has turned fully mainstream with retail frenzy and sell-side chasing nuclear stocks broadly. Catalyst_resolved = true forces thesis_live = false. The early-mover window was compressed to zero before a position could even be established.
2024-10-18.exit_advice: Exit CCJ immediately. The specific catalyst — the named deal signing — occurred before entry date and is now saturating mainstream financial press. No early-mover edge remains.

2024-12-20.thesis: Oklo signs named data-center nuclear power supply deal
2024-12-20.exit_case: Named data-center power deal announced December 18 — specific signed catalyst is already public and resolved.
2024-12-20.assessment: First week of tracking. The Investors.com article confirms Oklo announced a data-center power deal on December 18, 2024 ('Oklo announced a data center deal Wednesday'). The entry date of December 20 means the position was established *after* the catalyst had already occurred and been made public. Per the resolution rule, a named deal being signed/announced IS the catalyst resolving — the binary is behind us. Oklo may continue to benefit from nuclear sentiment, future deal flow, or regulatory milestones, but those are separate, unscheduled, speculative forward events — not the concrete discrete catalyst entered on. No pending scheduled next step on this specific deal is identified. Catalyst_resolved = true; thesis_live = false.
2024-12-20.exit_advice: Exit OKLO. The discrete catalyst — Oklo signing a named data-center nuclear power supply deal — occurred December 18, two days before entry. The event is public, announced, and priced. No further concrete scheduled binary remains on this specific deal. The event-driven edge is gone.
